details.text: Derivative: وَ: The وَ at the beginning of the Surah is وَاوُ القَسْم (the Waw of an oath i.e., I swear by). When a verse beginning with وَاوُ القَسْم is followed by verse beginning with فَ (then) it means that the verse is also subject to the وَاوُ القَسْم of the verse preceding it. Here, in this Surah, the two verses following this verse are subject to its oath as they both begin with فَ.

وَالصَّافَّاتِ صَفًّا

وَالصَّافَّاتِ صَفًّا– “Those lined up in rows or arranged in rows” according to the majority of the scholars of Tafsir refers to the مَلائكَة (Angels) who worship in rows or line up their wings in rows in the heavens awaiting commands to be given to them. صَافَّات uses the ألِف and تاء (اَت) can be used in Arabic to imply a small group of the plural known as جَمْعُ القِلَّة (The plural of a small number or minority). This indicates that the Angels are arranged in rows of small groups according to rank or purpose; one lined up after the other (صَفًّا).
Derivative: خَطِفَ to snatch away. In this context to snatch away a snatch’s worth (i.e., small portion) of information.
Derivative: ثَاقِبٌ piercing and penetrating. i.e., it penetrates the heaven to target the rebellious Shaytan.
Derivative: شِهَابٌ is a flame, blaze or a blazing meteor or shooting star.
Derivative: أَتْبَعَ to follow right behind or after.

Derivative: حَلِيمٍ carries the meaning of being caring, compassionate, patient, tolerant and loving, which can be understood as being a high level of sensitivity. حِلْم from which حَلِيمٍ is derived implies the characteristic of not being overwhelmed but rather you approach and deal with matters in a calm manner.
- يَا أَبَتِ is an expression of endearment, Like “O, my dear beloved father”.
Derivative: تَلَّ means here to bring to the ground.
Derivative: الْبَلَاءُ is a great test and trial. It is used in Arabic to refer to a calamity and traumatic experience.
Derivative: فَدَيْنَا from فَدَى – فِدْيَة which means to ransom and here it means “to exchange”.
Derivative: الْمُسْتَبِينَ That which has reached the furthest point in terms of clarity in reference to the Tawrah.
Derivative: عَجِبْتَ from عَجِبَ to be in a state of wonder or astonishment.
Derivative: إِلْ يَاسِينَ – It is said that this refers to the aforementioned Messenger إِلْيَاسَ (Ilyaas) and it is also said that it refers to him alongside those who followed him.
Derivative: مُّصْبِحِينَ means during your morning i.e., when you are on your travels during the morning. Morning can also refer more generally to the daytime.

- Normally مَرَّ is used with بِ to mean: To pass by. Here, مَرَّ is used with عَلَى to men: To pass over. This is as they have been erased entirely and they are literally passing over them and where they used to exist.
Derivative: أَبَقَ is a word meaning to flee and run away but is specifically used when a slave runs away from his master.

Derivative: الْمُدْحَضِينَ from أدْحَضَ which means to be defeated. Also, it means to be made to fall down, like the refutation of an argument to make it fall down, or here in this case, to be made to fall down off the ship i.e., الْمُدْحَضِينَ refers to those who were thrown overboard.
Derivative: سَاهَمَ means to cast or draw lots and also to participate in something.
Derivative: الْتَقَمَ means to swallow quickly or in one go.
Derivative: مُلِيمٌ – To be blameworthy i.e., referring to his leaving of the town and boarding a ship which was a blameworthy act.
Derivative: الْعَرَاءِ – A land that is empty and stripped of vegetation and buildings i.e., a desolate barren piece of land.
Derivative: سُلْطَانٌ – Authority or authoritative proof.
Derivative: دَاخِرُونَ from دَخَرَ which means to made small, humbled, humiliated and submissive.

Derivative: زَجْرَةٌ – A scolding, a single cry or shout (صَيْحَة) that puts an end to everything. It is said that a shepherd زَجَرَ his sheep, which means to cry or shout out to them to prevent them from something.

- فَالزَّاجِرَاتِ زَجْرًا – This continues to refer to the مَلائكَة (Angels). زَجَرَ means to drive or push away with force, to restrain or rebuke. The scholars of Tafsir have stated that it either refers to the Angels driving the winds with force or to the Angels rebuking the sinful acts by exhortation and advice. It has also been said to refer to the restrictions and preventions of the Qur’an which forbid the ugly acts.
Derivative: أَزْوَاجَ can refer to spouses or to peers and those who are similar in terms of characteristics and types.

- “From the (direction of the) right” is an expression to mean: From a position of power and influence, or a place that we trusted and followed in relation to the Deen and truth i.e., a source that we relied upon and trusted.
- فَالتَّالِيَاتِ ذِكْرًا – The Angels reciting the Dhikr (reminder) referring to the speech of Allah and the Qur’an.

- إِنَّ إِلَٰهَكُمْ لَوَاحِدٌ “Verily, your إِلَه (Deity worthy of worship and obedience) is One”: This statement is the subject of the oath at the beginning of the Surah i.e., I swear by the Angels arranged in rows, who drive the winds and recite the Dhikr, that verily your Rabb (Lord and master) is One.
Derivative: الْمُخْلَصِينَ – مُخْلَص (Mukhlas) is someone who has been granted sincerity (i.e., especially chosen by Allah) whilst مُخْلِص (Mukhlis) is someone who is sincere.
Derivative: النَّعِيمِ refers to constant favour, luxury, comfort and blessing (bliss).

Derivative: غَوْلٌ from غَالَ which means to take someone unawares and also to kill something. غَوْلٌ here refers to the damaging effects of alcohol in the life of the Dunya and particularly to those which effect the mind like headaches.
Derivative: عِينٌ means with beautiful wide and large eyes.
- “Those females who restrict or confine the eye” i.e., to their spouses. Another understanding is that it means to lower the eyes out of shyness.

- بَيْضٌ مَّكْنُونٌ literally means: “A concealed or covered egg”. However, this has been said to be referring to the egg of an ostrich which is covered by its feathers and preserves its egg with its pure whiteness. As such, it is a metaphor for the beauty of the colour of the eyes of the women of Jannah. Another understanding is that the مَّكْنُونٌ comes from كَنَّ with the meaning of being in the indent of a mountain. Here, the egg that is found, such as that of an eagle that has its nest in the indent of the mountain, is a metaphor for a hidden treasure.
Derivative: قَرِينٌ from قَرَنَ. قَرَنَ means to come up next to something, to connect or join to it. قَرِينٌ is someone who is closely connected or attached i.e., a companion.
Derivative: مَدِينُونَ means to be recompensed, accounted and judged for our actions.
Derivative: مُّطَّلِعُونَ from اِطَّلَع which means to climb up a height to discover something. It refers to investigating, looking into something and acquiring information.
Derivative: سَوَاءِ in relation to a place or time means the middle of it.
Derivative: تُرْدِي from أَرْدَى which means to make someone fall from a high place to the ground i.e., to bring about his downfall and ruin.
- The oath with تَاء in تَاللَّهِ is used in cases of emergency in exceptional cases and serious situations.
Derivative: الْمُحْضَرِينَ from أَحْضَرَ which means to be brought forth and presented. It is referring to the criminal who is brought forward to receive his recompense of hell.
Derivative: الْكَوَاكِبِ is the plural of كَوْكَب and refers to large stars and planets.

Derivative: الزَّقُّومِ is the tree in hell which is the source of food for its inhabitants. It is hideous, disgusting and unpalatable in every way. It provides no satisfaction or relief to those who consume from it.
Derivative: حَمِيمٍ refers to scalding water, water that has reached the extremities of hotness.
Derivative: شَوْبًا means a mix or mixture i.e., combination of ingredients.
Derivative: مَّارِدٍ from مَرَدَ which means to race to evil deeds and those who are impudent and rebellious.
Derivative: يُهْرَعُونَ – to rush and hurry i.e., they hasten to follow in their ancestors footsteps.
Derivative: الْكَرْبِ is a disturbance that causes distress and fear.

- The use of “هُمْ” here in: “And we made his offspring, them (هُمْ) those who remain and continue” adds the meaning of restriction and limitation (الحَصْر) and indicates that all those of mankind who came after are from the offspring of his (Nuh’s (as)) offspring.

Derivative: يَسَّمَّعُونَ is from اِسَّمَّعَ and means to make effort to listen to something or someone secretly or to eavesdrop i.e., they try to gather information or intelligence from the Higher assembly of Angels but are unable to.
Derivative: شِيعَتِهِ – شِيعَة is a group consisting of those who share the same beliefs. Ibrahim (as) is from the same شِيعَة as Nuh (as) in terms of the fundamentals of the Deen.

- بِقَلْبٍ سَلِيمٍ means with a sound, good, clean and healthy heart. It is free of diseases and insincerities. Even the sound mind (عَقْل سَليم) or a genius mind will not bring its possessor to the truth if he does not have a قَلْبٍ سَلِيمٍ (sound heart).
Derivative: وَاصِبٌ which means perpetual, lasting and constant.
Derivative: دُحُورًا from دَحَرَ which means to be drive away and expel. دُحُورًا means driven off.

- وَمَا تَعْمَلُونَ can mean “And what you make” referring to the idols they carve. It can also be said to have a wider understanding related to actions in general i.e., “And what you do”. For example, Ar-Raazi states that it refers to the consequences and results of actions, in the case where a person only controls his initial act but not the results of that act.
